data: Funerary inscription for Marcus Avianius Pistianus, Maria Eupraxia and Marius Eutychianus
Marble plaque, with some pitting to the upper part of the front face, corrosion to the lower front face, and some damage / loss to the lower right corner; the text is complete.
The text is set out over 8 lines, approximately centred, decreasing in size from top to bottom.
use of serifs and interpuncts, with some instances of tall and small letters, and ligatures.
later first or second century CE
Found in Centuripe and sold to Palermo museum in 1879 by one sig. Salvatore Fallica di Catania
Partially reopened following extensive restoration work. Comprehensive collection of archaeological material, predominantly from the West of the island, with an important Etruscan collection.
Centre of Palermo, Piazza Olivella 24, 90100 - Palermo
Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae

T. Mommsen, Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae, Corpus Inscriptionum Latinarum, consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae editum, 10.2 (Berlin: G. Reimer, 1883)
Iscrizioni latine lapidarie del Museo di Palermo
Livia Bivona, Iscrizioni latine lapidarie del Museo di Palermo, Sikelia 5 (Palermo: Flaccovio, 1970)
La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano
Giacomo Manganaro, «La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano», Aufstieg und Niedergang der romischen Welt 2.11.1 (1988): 3–89
Jonathan Prag revised metadata from published material, added translation and checked against photograph
Manganaro, 1988, La Sicilia da Sesto Pompeo a Diocleziano

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of CIL checked against photograph

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Sacred to the shades of the departed. Marcus Avianus Pistianus and Maria Eupraxia, set this up for themselves and their family while they still lived, and for their most dutiful son Marius Eutychianus, undeserving of an early death, who lived 17 years, 11 months, 16 days.
